title: Neon Genesis Evangelion and the Japanese Monster Movie
display_title: Neon Genesis Evangelion and the Japanese Monster Movie
wikitext:
[]: Neon Genesis Evangelion and the Japanese Monster Movie             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Fri Oct 10, 2008 2:34 pm Not sure if this has been covered in any detail before (and I really don't feel like wading through pages and pages of threads), but here goes. This past weekend, Svengoolie played one of the Mothra movies. This particular entry featured two minature girls being kept inside a cage by some "bad guys" who I believe wanted to sell their stolen good to the highest bidder, and Mothra invaded/destroyed Japan because he wanted them returned--I'm not sure why all this was happening; I only started watching about halfway through (one of those happy accidents that occurs whilst channel-surfing). Anyway, today I was thinking about Evangelion , and it hit me that the Angels serve much the same purpose as Mothra and monsters of his ilk. That is to say, the monsters represent the cultural gap between mankind and nature--and, therefore, the gap between individuals, represented by the AT Field. Through this post, we can see how Evangelion follows in the tradition of the Japanese monster movie, an example of which is playing at the nearly-abandoned multiplex Shinji happens upon in "Hedgehog's Dilemma" (Episode 4). The Monster, who represents nature, enters the City because he's looking for something that we've wrongfully taken, and the humans go in to attack him, thinking him evil and something to be destroyed, when in fact he just wants his favorite teddy bear (or whatever) back, and the City just happens to be in his path. His only problems are A) he's too big and clumsy and B) being a Monster, he lacks the social graces we humans take for granted; namely communication. Needless, the humans on the attack are more often than not the ones who have something to gain from their ill-gotten goods, and who are so blinded by their own goals that they don't see it from the other side. BUT WAIT! The Angels go one step further than Mothra and other assorted delights in that they're both looking for their "long-lost teddy bear" (I'm opening myself up to Gollum jokes here) AND they're looking to say, "Hi, we come from far away and we just want to be friends" (which is my take on the events of "Don't Be."--Arael's attempt at communication with Asuka is [mis]interpreted as its preferred method of attack. Either that, or, since the Angels are meant to be different evolutionary paths that we COULD have taken, they're looking at us in awe, and are genuinely interested in what makes us tick). Unfortunately, since the Angels have been wrongfully designated the "bad guys" of Evangelion (i.e. they're the bad guys to the human characters) due to their...unorthodox appearance and characteristics, the human characters--who we later find out have intruded upon the Angels' territory [I think] and starting Second Impact as a result--simply will not give the Angels the time of day! (We're starting to go into thoughts of prejudice here, but I'll open that up for discussion) -------------------------------------------- That's pretty much all I have to say on the subject. Please forgive me if I'm pulling stuff out of thin air, or am inaccurate on some of the details, but I'm simply going on what I DO know having watched the entire series...and not a lot is made perfectly clear within it. By the way, I didn't read any of the articles on the Wiki before doing this; I'm in an analytical mood today, and this was an idea that just popped into my head.[/i]

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 10, 2008 3:26 pm You look over the fact that if the Angels get their Mommy back, then Lilim are rendered extinct.

[]: Re: Neon Genesis Evangelion and the Japanese Monster Movie             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Fri Oct 10, 2008 6:29 pm Sharaz Destler wrote: Unfortunately, since the Angels have been wrongfully designated the "bad guys" of Evangelion (i.e. they're the bad guys to the human characters) due to their...unorthodox appearance and characteristics, the human characters--who we later find out have intruded upon the Angels' territory [I think] and starting Second Impact as a result--simply will not give the Angels the time of day! (We're starting to go into thoughts of prejudice here, but I'll open that up for discussion)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Oct 10, 2008 8:31 pm That works well for Sachiel. Zeruel however is a genuine asshole.

[]: Re: Neon Genesis Evangelion and the Japanese Monster Movie             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Fri Oct 10, 2008 10:21 pm AuraTwilight wrote: You look over the fact that if the Angels get their Mommy back, then Lilim are rendered extinct.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Oct 11, 2008 1:54 pm If we overlook the fact that Angels merging with Adam causes 3I, there are quite a few Angelic character types that can be explored here. Zeruel, as NemZ so aptly put, is almost certainly an aggressor. Tabris (Kaworu) is also fully aware of his actions' consequences, but he's an exception since he was a SEELE creation. I think Sachiel started off meaning no harm, then gradually got annoyed at all the bombings and the shootings. He also may have seen EVA-01, a clone of Adam, as a being meaning to kill him, or at least a potential threat, since AT Fields would have betrayed the pilot's true intentions. (Well, that's what I think anyways.) [Insert AT Field-related fanwank here] Shamshel probably got annoyed at being bullet-sprayed; Ramiel, seeing that fates of his two companions, decided to pre-empt his enemy (and did a damn good job of it before Misato came up with Operation Yashima). I know I'm assuming the Angels know about each other, but Armisael pretty much proves that point, IMO. Israfel I'm not sure about. Perhaps the attack was a reaction to getting shot at and chopped in half; perhaps it was intended the whole time. I dunno. Gaghiel and Sandalphon were probs just having a nice swim (Sandalphon's actions particularly can easily be interpreted as infantile thrashing); Matarael, Sahaquiel and Leliel were just trying to get to Adam/Lilith; we know that Ireul was most likely have trouble simply surviving , until it hacked the Magi and made the decision to fight back. It's no surprise that Zeruel and Bardiel, having seen the carnage, decided to take matters into their own hands. We also know that the last three Angels present to us a trio of communicative types: Arael, Armisael and Tabris. Rather than attack directly, they attempted communicating with one of each of the pilots, with various results. The rest is history. Ultimately, though, I think the Angels that knew what they were doing did it out of a compassion for humanity. It's only natural that if we were suffering and massacring each other, they should want to put us out of our misery; lacking the suitable communicative tools to do it, they resorted to force.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Sat Oct 11, 2008 2:08 pm You can also take you're whole theory, and apply it to Commando . Renegade soldiers wrongfully kidnap Arnold's kid, and Arnold goes to look for her. Upon his approach to the island where the daughter is held, his huge form and blatant ammo-ridden body lead the soldiers to presuming that he is the enemy and attack him, whereas as the governor of California, his only knowledge of communication is inaccurately perceived as throwing grenades at groups of soldiers, when in fact he's simply trying to ask where his daughter is in the form of explosions.

[]:             Quote Post by Silent Lightning » Sat Oct 11, 2008 2:10 pm Must be annoying for a military guy...

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Oct 11, 2008 4:54 pm Truth be told I'm actually surprised that more of the Angels weren't outright hostile towards humanity, especially with the way most of them calmly ignored signs of human presence until attacked (or until Evas showed up). If anything, they probably care more about humanity than we think they do.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Oct 12, 2008 1:51 pm Or just don't care at all. All they care about is returning to Adam. Everything else is just scenery or something in the way.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Oct 13, 2008 10:52 am AuraTwilight wrote: Or just don't care at all. All they care about is returning to Adam. Everything else is just scenery or something in the way.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Oct 13, 2008 3:46 pm Sharaz Destler wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: Or just don't care at all. All they care about is returning to Adam. Everything else is just scenery or something in the way.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Mon Oct 13, 2008 4:25 pm *Dancing in the background* That's - because - we - have - the - fruit - of - KNOWLEEEEEEEEEEEDGE!

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Mon Oct 13, 2008 4:51 pm Am I supposed to take Kaworu as an exception to the rule, then?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 14, 2008 8:44 am AyrYntake wrote: Am I supposed to take Kaworu as an exception to the rule, then?

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Tue Oct 14, 2008 4:56 pm AuraTwilight wrote: The way the Angels behave, compared with Kaworu's monologue, implies that they'd be returning to Adam either way due to instinctual compulsion. Angels are destined to return to Mother. Lilith they don't really give a damn about.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Tue Oct 14, 2008 10:57 pm I dunno, Leliel, Armisael, and Arael all seem to take an interest in humans.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Oct 15, 2008 3:21 am Lucretius wrote: I dunno, Leliel, Armisael, and Arael all seem to take an interest in humans.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Wed Oct 15, 2008 8:57 am AuraTwilight wrote: AyrYntake wrote: Am I supposed to take Kaworu as an exception to the rule, then?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 15, 2008 11:54 am Actually, it's very possible that Gaghiel wasn't just drawn to/looking for Adam but was also drawn to Eva-02 since the Eva are Adam-based life. (With the exception of Eva-01 who's surrogate is Lilith even though all Eva come from Adam and blah blah blah.)
